headline_summary: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed; IRGC warns it will fire on any ship attempting transit
Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports; restart expected to take weeks
Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge (some >1000%) as Gulf conflict widens and Hormuz traffic is paralyzed
Hezbollah launches missiles/drones at Israel; Israel sends troops into south Lebanon and strikes Beirut
Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say
risk_drivers: Explicit Strait of Hormuz closure threat / de facto traffic paralysis
Attacks on tankers, ports, and energy infrastructure across Gulf
LNG supply shock from Qatar force majeure
Multi-theatre escalation (Lebanon/Israel; Iraq attacks on US facilities)
Surging maritime insurance and cyber-retaliation risk

Energy: Higher crude risk premium; backwardation/volatility risk if Hormuz disruption persists.
LNG/European gas upside risk due to Qatari supply outage and constrained rerouting.
FX: EM oil-importer FX downside risk; USD/JPY and CHF bid on risk-off; Gulf pegs watched for reserve drawdowns.
Rates: Inflation shock risk lifts breakevens; front-end cuts repriced; credit spreads can widen on risk-off.
Equities: Risk-off tilt: energy/defense outperform; airlines/chemicals/transport underperform; higher vol.

sources.title: Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Exclusive: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction, will take weeks to restart, sources say
Maritime insurance premiums surge as Iran conflict widens
Lebanon sucked deeper into war as Hezbollah, Israel trade blows
Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say
